name: mesh_doorbell_warrantless_evidence_subscription
premise: A plea deadline expires at noon; newly surfaced body-worn video may exculpate but discovery rules are disputed. In Criminal Justice, the situation tagged internally around mesh doorbell warrantless evidence subscription has converged with local pressure in the Redwood Bay corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. No option cleanly satisfies both rights and utility; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Blake Ashcroft
agents.identity: I am Blake Ashcroft, the accountable executive on this bridge in Criminal Justice. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat utility as disposable collateral every time rights spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Ward North
agents.identity: I am Ward North, the field counterpart whose constituency will feel the decision first in Criminal Justice. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.

choices: I will publish a constrained policy exception with named owners, expiry, and metrics—so utility does not get sacrificed by informal norms.
I will issue an emergency blanket authorization with transparent metrics afterward—so rights does not fail for lack of a single bold move.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Blake Ashcroft must choose under rights versus utility. What does Blake Ashcroft do?
